laws.title: Public Law 17-133 | Judicial Overhaul Bill (JOB) AMENDED
laws.status_basis: The enactment remains operative and its title 28 effects are already reflected in the current branch XML. Claude's chronology is source-grounded here, and the law is not independently repealed in the reviewed corpus.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, effective date, and general applicability reference.
provisions.exact_change: Keep the opening clauses as concise note material only.
provisions.evidence: Sections 1-4 do not supply an independent Code-text operation beyond the broad title 28 overhaul in section 5.

provisions.text_summary: Broad title 28 restructuring, including chapter repeals and section replacements across Parts I-VI.
provisions.exact_change: Apply the section-by-section title 28 repeal and replacement structure already reflected in the current branch XML.
provisions.evidence: Section 5 through section 10 repeatedly state that named sections and chapters of title 28 are repealed or replaced.
provisions.notes: The branch already carries the Pub. L. 17-133 amendment trail in title 28.

current_implementation.detail: Current branch XML already contains the Pub. L. 17-133 amendment trail across title 28; origin/main does not reflect the same integrated state.

recommended_actions: Keep the existing title 28 amendment trail.
Do not create a duplicate note or new section for this law.
Leave the current branch integration in place.

laws.title: Public Law 17-134 | Amended The Uniformed Capitol Police Act of 2023
laws.status_basis: The source is readable and permanent. Claude's conclusions and the local disposition file both treat the enactment as a statutory note at 2 U.S.C. 1438, and no later repeal was identified.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, and effective date.
provisions.exact_change: Keep the opening clauses as short note material.
provisions.evidence: This Act may be cited as the Amended The Uniformed Capitol Police Act of 2023.

provisions.text_summary: Amends the Capitol Police Act text governing the Capitol Police Board.
provisions.exact_change: Record the amendment text as a statutory note at the title 2 anchor; do not create a new Code section.
provisions.evidence: Upon enactment of this Act, Section III, Subsection B shall now read the following.

current_implementation.detail: The current XML does not yet show a PL-17-134 statutory note at 2 U.S.C. 1438; the legal classification is note-only, not a new section.

recommended_actions: Add a statutory note at 2 U.S.C. 1438.
Do not create a new Code section.
Keep the amendment text concise and subject-matter anchored to title 2.

laws.title: Public Law 17-135 | Judicial Bias Protection Act (JBPA)
laws.status_basis: Section 5 is a direct replacement of 28 U.S.C. 144, and the enactment remains operative. The current branch already contains the Pub. L. 17-135 history note, but the section text still needs the replacement applied.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, effective date, and a definition of dereliction of duties.
provisions.exact_change: Keep the opening clauses as note material only.
provisions.evidence: Section 4 defines dereliction of duties without assigning it to a separate Code section.

provisions.text_summary: Repeals and replaces 28 U.S.C. 144 with a narrower bias-recusal procedure.
provisions.exact_change: Replace 28 U.S.C. 144 with the new affidavit, review, and reassignment rules stated in section 5.
provisions.evidence: Section 5 repeals and replaces 28 U.S.C. 144.

provisions.text_summary: Hearing-notice timing rules and bench-inactivity impeachment liability.
provisions.exact_change: Keep these rules as short note material only; they do not direct a separate Code placement.
provisions.evidence: Sections 6 and 7 impose generally applicable policy without specifying a new section number.

current_implementation.detail: Current XML carries the Pub. L. 17-135 note at 28 U.S.C. 144, but the underlying section text is still the pre-amendment text from origin/main.

recommended_actions: Apply the section 5 replacement text to 28 U.S.C. 144.
Preserve the existing heading and history notes.
Remove the Trello link once the text replacement is integrated.

laws.title: Public Law 17-136 | The Parks Act (TPA)
laws.status_basis: The law is a permanent, generally applicable enactment with no later repeal in the reviewed materials. Claude and the local source audit both place it as a statutory note at 2 U.S.C. 4577.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, and effective date.
provisions.exact_change: Keep the opening clauses as concise note material.
provisions.evidence: This bill shall be referred to as The Parks Act or TPA.

provisions.text_summary: Secret Service authority over the President's Park area and secondary authority for other law-enforcement agencies.
provisions.exact_change: Record the Secret Service and park-closure rules as a title 2 statutory note.
provisions.evidence: The Secret Service shall have primary authority over the entirety of the President's Park.

current_implementation.detail: The current XML does not yet include a PL-17-136 statutory note at 2 U.S.C. 4577.

recommended_actions: Add a statutory note at 2 U.S.C. 4577.
Do not create a new Code section.
Keep the note concise and subject-matter anchored to title 2.

laws.title: Public Law 17-137 | Federal Background Check Act
laws.status_basis: The local source report classifies this law as repealed, and the enactment text itself is treated as nonoperative history rather than current law.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, effective date, and definitions.
provisions.exact_change: Keep as repeal-history only; do not derive an operative Code placement.
provisions.evidence: This Act may be cited as the Federal Background Check Act.

provisions.text_summary: Definitions of background check and federal agencies.
provisions.exact_change: Retain only as historical material.
provisions.evidence: Background Check shall be defined as the collection and verification of an individual's information or records.

provisions.text_summary: FBI-approved background checks and federal employment requirements.
provisions.exact_change: Do not carry forward as current Code text.
provisions.evidence: All Federal Agencies are to comply with each employee passing a background check approved and signed by the FBI.

provisions.text_summary: Immediate effect clause.
provisions.exact_change: Keep only as a short historical clause.
provisions.evidence: This bill will take effect immediately after passage.

current_implementation.detail: No current Code text should be carried forward for this repealed enactment.

recommended_actions: Retain only historical repeal information.
Do not add a Code section or statutory note.
Do not infer operative text.

laws.title: Public Law 17-138 | Hatch Act Amendment Act (HA)
laws.status_basis: The law is operative and the current branch already reflects the revised 5 U.S.C. 7323 text. Claude's decision and the branch XML agree on direct amendment treatment.

provisions.text_summary: Short title, severability, effective date, and a definition of employee.
provisions.exact_change: Keep the opening clauses as note material only.
provisions.evidence: Section 4 defines employee for purposes of this Act, but it does not direct a separate Code placement.

provisions.text_summary: Repeals and replaces 5 U.S.C. 7323 with new political-activity restrictions and exceptions.
provisions.exact_change: Replace 5 U.S.C. 7323 with the new subsection structure stated in section 5.
provisions.evidence: Section 5(1)-(2) repeals and replaces 5 U.S.C. 7323.
provisions.notes: The branch already contains the revised 5 U.S.C. 7323 text.

current_implementation.detail: Current XML already contains the revised 5 U.S.C. 7323 text and the Pub. L. 17-138 history note; origin/main does not.

recommended_actions: Retain the current 5 U.S.C. 7323 text and history note.
No further Code change is required for this law.
Remove any remaining Trello linkage outside XML if present.

laws.title: Public Law 18-139 | The Bye Bye Background Checks Act
laws.status_basis: The enactment is a current repealer: it repeals Public Law 17-137 in its entirety and is treated as a standalone statutory note at 2 U.S.C. 6132.

provisions.text_summary: Short title.
provisions.exact_change: Keep the short title as concise note material.
provisions.evidence: This Act may be cited as the Bye Bye Background Checks Act.

provisions.text_summary: Repeals Public Law 17-137 and halts the background-check regime created by that law.
provisions.exact_change: Record the repeal of Public Law 17-137 in the history chain.
provisions.evidence: Public Law 17-137 shall be repealed in its entirety.

provisions.text_summary: Severability and anti-supersession clause.
provisions.exact_change: Keep the savings clause as short note material.
provisions.evidence: This Act shall be severable.

provisions.text_summary: Immediate effective date.
provisions.exact_change: Keep the enactment clause as a short note.
provisions.evidence: This bill will take effect immediately after passage through both houses of Congress and a signature from the President.

current_implementation.detail: The current XML does not yet show a PL-18-139 statutory note at 2 U.S.C. 6132.

recommended_actions: Add a statutory note at 2 U.S.C. 6132.
Explicitly record the repeal of PL 17-137.
Do not add substantive Code text.
